_provenance.paper: Blaney HF, Criddle WD (1950) Determining water requirements in irrigated areas from climatological and irrigation data. USDA-SCS Tech Paper 96.
supplementary: Doorenbos J, Pruitt WO (1977) FAO-24 Crop water requirements. Table 18.
Allen RG, Pereira LS (1998) FAO-56, Annex to Ch 2 (Blaney-Criddle as reference).
Xu CY, Singh VP (2001) Evaluation of three complementary models. J Hydrol 242:95-107.
_provenance.data_source: Published equations and FAO-24 Table 18 (open literature)

notes: p = mean daily percentage of annual daytime hours, depends on latitude and month
FAO-24 adds a + b correction for humidity, wind, and sunshine ratio
Original (1950) is uncorrected; widely used in western US irrigation districts

monthly_daylight_pct.description: Mean daily percentage of annual daytime hours (p) by latitude and month, from FAO-24 Table 18
monthly_daylight_pct.note: p values sum to ~100% over 12 months for each latitude. Each value is percent per DAY for that month.

analytical_benchmarks.name: negative_temperature
analytical_benchmarks.description: Edge case: Tmean=-10°C, p=0.222 — formula goes negative, clamped to 0

analytical_benchmarks.name: very_cold
analytical_benchmarks.description: Edge case: Tmean=-20°C, p=0.199 — inner term near zero

daylight_computation.description: p can be computed from latitude and day-of-year using FAO-56 solar equations
daylight_computation.formula: p = N / (365 × 12) where N = (24/π) × ωs, ωs = arccos(-tan(φ) × tan(δ))
daylight_computation.notes: For monthly averages, use mid-month DOY

cross_method_comparison.description: Compare BC with PM, PT, HG at lat=42.7°N (Michigan), summer
cross_method_comparison.notes: BC tends to overestimate in humid climates (no humidity correction in original)

monotonicity_checks.name: temperature_monotonic
monotonicity_checks.description: Higher T → higher ET₀ at constant p

monotonicity_checks.name: daylight_monotonic
monotonicity_checks.description: Higher p → higher ET₀ at constant T

monotonicity_checks.name: latitude_seasonal
monotonicity_checks.description: Summer p > winter p for northern latitudes
